food_subgroup: Ice cream sundae with sauce topping

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic 'ice cream sundae' class (FOODON_03301116). No specific mandarin orange or hot fudge variant exists; closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Ice cream and similar frozen desserts
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX covers ice cream and similar frozen desserts. No specific sundae sub-code exists; this is the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for ice cream sundae with flavoured sauce found; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard McDonald's sundae cup, approximately 170 g, consisting of soft-serve ice cream topped with hot fudge sauce and mandarin orange flavouring.

nutrient_source.title: Ice Cream Sundae with Fudge Topping — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168892 and composite estimate)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for 'Mandarin Orange Hot Fudge Sundae' exists. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for 'Ice cream sundae with fudge topping' (FDC ID 168892) as the closest defensible analogue. Mandarin orange flavouring contributes negligible macro change at typical usage levels. Values represent a composite soft-serve ice cream base with hot fudge sauce at approximately 60:40 ratio by weight. Confidence is medium due to product-specific formulation not being publicly disclosed by McDonald's Singapore.

gi_source.title: Ice cream (vanilla, full-fat) — Glycemic Index Database; and hot fudge sauce composite estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 'Mandarin Orange Hot Fudge Sundae'. GI of 47 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for full-fat vanilla ice cream (GI ~47, low category), which is the dominant component by weight. Hot fudge sauce (high sugar, moderate fat) has a blunted GI response due to fat content. Mandarin orange flavouring is present in trace amounts and does not materially alter GI. Composite GI estimated at 47 (low). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (47 × 30.5 g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.70 serving weight) / 100 = approximately 24.3 g GL per serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~95 mg per 100 g; ~162 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single serving contributes approximately 162 mg sodium, which is modest relative to the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; occasional consumption is unlikely to be clinically significant, but regular intake should be monitored in the context of overall dietary sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~24.8 g sugar per 100 g; ~42 g per serving) and significant carbohydrate load
type2_diabetes.guidance: With approximately 42 g of sugar per serving and a glycemic load of ~24 g, this dessert poses a high postprandial glucose risk for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be consumed only occasionally, with careful carbohydrate budgeting within the meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.6 g per 100 g; ~6.1 g per serving) and added sugar contributing to atherogenic risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each serving provides approximately 6.1 g of saturated fat, representing roughly 28–31% of the 20 g daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should limit consumption of this high-sugar, moderate-saturated-fat dessert.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Mandarin Orange Hot Fudge Sundae is a limited-edition or seasonal soft-serve ice cream dessert sold at McDonald's Singapore outlets, including locations at Springleaf Tower and Boat Quay. It consists of a standard McDonald's soft-serve vanilla ice cream base dispensed into a cup, topped with a warm, silky hot fudge chocolate sauce, and finished with a bright mandarin orange flavouring that provides a citrus-forward contrast to the rich chocolate. The texture combines the cold, creamy softness of the ice cream with the smooth, slightly viscous warmth of the fudge sauce. The flavour profile is sweet and indulgent, with the mandarin orange element adding a fruity, slightly tangy note that cuts through the richness of the chocolate. Nutritionally, a single serving (~170 g) provides approximately 315 kcal, 42 g of sugar, 9.9 g of fat (6.1 g saturated), and 51.9 g of carbohydrate. The glycemic load per serving is approximately 24 g, placing it in the high glycemic load category despite the ice cream base having a low-to-medium GI (~47). This dessert is classified as ultra-processed and is high in added sugars, making it a high-concern food for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or following a low-sugar diet. It is halal-certified at McDonald's Singapore and is suitable for vegetarians. It contains dairy and soy allergens. The dish is priced at SGD 3.45 and is available at participating McDonald's Singapore restaurants.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional data for McDonald's Singapore Mandarin Orange Hot Fudge Sundae is publicly available from an approved primary source. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest USDA SR Legacy analogue (ice cream sundae with fudge topping, FDC ID 168892). GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for full-fat vanilla ice cream. Confidence is medium because the product-specific formulation, including the mandarin orange flavouring component, is not disclosed in any approved source database.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 168892, ice cream sundae with fudge topping) as the closest defensible analogue. McDonald's Singapore does not publish full nutritional data for this specific seasonal variant in any approved source database. 2. GI of 47 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for full-fat vanilla ice cream; this is the dominant component. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy analogue and are not confirmed from a product-specific source; these values carry medium confidence. 4. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record; all micronutrient fields are populated with estimated values from the USDA analogue source. 5. The cuisine tag 'Burger' from the input field survey record reflects the McDonald's restaurant category tag and does not accurately describe this dessert product; reclassified as 'Fast Food / Western Dessert' for this record. 6. Halal status is based on McDonald's Singapore's general halal certification (MUIS-certified); individual product halal status should be verified against current MUIS records. 7. Serving size of 170 g is estimated based on typical McDonald's sundae cup fill weight; no official weight is disclosed for this specific product.

improvement_actions_taken: Reclassified cuisine from 'Burger' (field survey restaurant category tag) to 'Fast Food / Western Dessert' to accurately reflect the food type.
Added mandatory multilingual local_names for Singapore context: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Populated all per_100g nutrient fields using USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 168892 as closest defensible analogue.
Estimated GI of 47 from University of Sydney GI Database (full-fat vanilla ice cream) as dominant component analogue.
Added comprehensive health_context for Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and Cardiovascular Disease with specific clinical guidance.
Populated llm_training section with >200-character natural language description, common user queries, meal_context, and ingredient_synonyms.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency.
Documented all estimation and adaptation decisions in notes and nutrient_source fields.
Set confidence to 'medium' reflecting adapted rather than exact source match.
Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'close' to 'closest_parent' to align with VERITAS_SCORING_MODEL_v2.md requirements and resolve G5 hard gate failure.
